steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Set up the conversion ratio between degrees and radians
steps.content:
The fundamental relationship between degrees and radians is:
$$180^{\circ} = \pi \text{ radians}$$
To convert any angle in degrees to radians, multiply by the conversion factor $\frac{\pi}{180}$.

steps.title: Apply the conversion formula to 60 degrees
steps.content:
Multiply 60° by the conversion factor:
$$60^{\circ} \times \frac{\pi}{180} = \frac{60\pi}{180}$$
Simplify by dividing both numerator and denominator by 60:
$$\frac{60\pi}{180} = \frac{\pi}{3} = \frac{1}{3}\pi$$
That confirms D is correct.
